definition: A Taiwan Strait crisis follows a three-phase correlation transition: initial safe-haven rally (negative correlation) → supply chain inflation overwhelms (transition) → persistent positive correlation. Options markets price Taiwan risk through equity vol but NOT through rate vol or cross-asset correlation, suggesting mispricing. Probability estimate of 8-12% over 3 years is acknowledged as false precision.
evidence: Three-phase model: safe-haven → supply chain inflation → persistent positive correlation
Options markets price Taiwan risk asymmetrically (equity vol only, not rate vol or cross-asset)
8-12% probability over 3 years, though acknowledged as imprecise
open_questions: Is the asymmetric options pricing a tradeable mispricing or an appropriate reflection of scenario uncertainty?
How does the three-phase model calibrate to historical geopolitical disruptions (e.g., 1990 Kuwait invasion)?
What early warning indicators would precede a crisis?
